results.title: Suffering
results.description: Unpleasantness, discontentment, dissatisfaction, stress, pain, disease, i.e. mild or intense suffering that is inherent in conditioned existence.

results.title: Dukkha sutta - Suffering
results.description: Six qualities to dwell in ease, without distress, without anguish - 1) thoughts of relinquishment, 2) non-ill will, 3) non-harming, 4) perceptions of relinquishment, 5) non-ill will, 6) non-harming.
results.contentSnippet: “Chahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu diṭṭheva dhamme <mark class="bg-yellow-100 dark:bg-yellow-900 rounded" style="padding-inline:0.05rem">dukkhaṁ</mark> viharati savighātaṁ saupāyāsaṁ sapa

results.title: Dukkhavihāra sutta - Discontented Abiding
results.description: A person endowed with the two qualities of guarding the sense doors and moderation in eating lives with discontentedness in this very life and after death, a bad destination is expected.
results.contentSnippet:
Bhojanamhi amattaññū,
indriyesu asaṁvuto;
Kāyadukkhaṁ cetodukkhaṁ,
<mark class="bg-yellow-100 dark:bg-yellow-900 rounded" style="padding-inline:0.05rem">dukkhaṁ</mark> so adhigacchati.

results.title: Dukkhanibbānasappāya sutta - Discontentment As Suitable For Realizing Nibbāna
results.description: The way of practice suitable for realizing Nibbāna is to see the six sense bases and their objects as sources of discontentment.
results.contentSnippet: Idha, bhikkhave, cakkhuṁ <mark class="bg-yellow-100 dark:bg-yellow-900 rounded" style="padding-inline:0.05rem">dukkhanti</mark> passati, rūpā dukkhāti passati, cakkhuviññāṇaṁ dukkhanti passati, cakkhu

results.title: Recognition of unsatisfactoriness
results.description: Perceiving the inherent inadequacy and unreliability of conditioned existence. This recognition loosens craving and the pursuit of lasting satisfaction in what cannot endure.

results.title: Vipallāsa sutta - Inversion
results.description: The Buddha describes these four inversions of perception, thought, and view, and the four non-inversions. An uninstructed ordinary person perceives permanence in the impermanent, pleasure in the unsat
results.contentSnippet: <mark class="bg-yellow-100 dark:bg-yellow-900 rounded" style="padding-inline:0.05rem">dukkhe</mark>, bhikkhave, dukkhanti nasaññāvipallāso nacittavipallāso nadiṭṭhivipallāso;

results.title: Titthāyatana sutta - Bases Of Sectarian Views
results.description: The Buddha explains the three bases of sectarian views that when closely examined, culminate in inaction. He then shares the Dhamma that is irrefutable, undefiled, blameless, and not disapproved of by

results.title: Mahā taṇhāsaṅkhaya sutta - The Greater Discourse on the Exhaustion of Craving

results.contentSnippet: So evaṁ anurodhavirodhaṁ samāpanno yaṁ kiñci vedanaṁ vedeti sukhaṁ vā <mark class="bg-yellow-100 dark:bg-yellow-900 rounded" style="padding-inline:0.05rem">dukkhaṁ</mark> vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā, so taṁ

results.title: Laṭukikopama sutta - Simile of the Quail
results.description: The Buddha explains how even small attachments can be strong fetters if not relinquished, using similes of a quail and an elephant, and contrasts between the poor and wealthy. He describes four types
results.contentSnippet: Idhudāyi, bhikkhu vivicceva kāmehi vivicca akusalehi dhammehi savitakkaṁ savicāraṁ vivekajaṁ pītisukhaṁ paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharati. Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhu vitakkavicārānaṁ vūpasamā ajjhattaṁ

results.title: Saccavibhaṅga sutta - Exposition of the Truths

results.contentSnippet: Katamañcāvuso, <mark class="bg-yellow-100 dark:bg-yellow-900 rounded" style="padding-inline:0.05rem">dukkhaṁ</mark> ariyasaccaṁ? Jātipi dukkhā, jarāpi dukkhā, maraṇampi dukkhaṁ, sokaparidevadukkhadoma

results.title: Vakkali sutta - Vakkali
results.description: “One who sees the Dhamma sees me.” When the dying Vakkali regrets not visiting the Master, the Buddha offers a radical correction: the physical body is not the Buddha. It ends with a dramatic search b
results.contentSnippet: Atha kho te bhikkhū yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho te bhikkhū bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ: “vakkali, bhante, bhikkhu ābādhiko <mark class="bg-yellow

results.title: Kesamutti sutta - With the Kālāmas of Kesamutta

results.contentSnippet: Etha tumhe, kālāmā, mā anussavena, mā paramparāya, mā itikirāya, mā piṭakasampadānena, mā takkahetu, mā nayahetu, mā ākāraparivitakkena, mā diṭṭhinijjhānakkhantiyā, mā bhabbarūpatāya, mā samaṇo no gar

results.title: Mahāsāropama sutta - The Greater Discourse on the Simile of the Heartwood
results.description: After Devadatta’s departure, the Buddha taught that the spiritual life is not pursued for acquisitions, respect, popularity, ethical conduct, collectedness, or knowledge and vision. Through the simile
results.contentSnippet: “Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco kulaputto saddhā agārasmā anagāriyaṁ pabbajito hoti: ‘otiṇṇomhi jātiyā jarāya maraṇena sokehi paridevehi <mark class="bg-yellow-100 dark:bg-yellow-900 rounded" style="padding-

results.title: Mahāsatipaṭṭhāna sutta - The Great Discourse on the Establishments of Mindfulness

results.contentSnippet: Jātipi <mark class="bg-yellow-100 dark:bg-yellow-900 rounded" style="padding-inline:0.05rem">dukkhā</mark>, jarāpi dukkhā, maraṇampi dukkhaṁ, sokaparidevadukkhadomanassupāyāsāpi dukkhā, appiyehi sampa

results.title: Cūḷasāropama sutta - The Shorter Discourse on the Simile of the Heartwood
results.description: When questioned about rival teachers, the Buddha sets the debate aside to explain the true purpose of the spiritual life through the simile of the heartwood. He cautions that settling for fame, ethica
results.contentSnippet: Evameva kho, brāhmaṇa, idhekacco puggalo saddhā agārasmā anagāriyaṁ pabbajito hoti: ‘otiṇṇomhi jātiyā jarāya maraṇena sokehi paridevehi <mark class="bg-yellow-100 dark:bg-yellow-900 rounded" style="pa

results.title: Mahāvedalla sutta - The Greater Series Of Questions And Answers
results.description: A series of questions and answers between Venerable Sāriputta and Venerable Mahākoṭṭhita that clarify subtle yet important aspects of the teachings. Topics covered include wisdom, consciousness, felt
results.contentSnippet: “‘Nappajānāti nappajānātī’ti kho, āvuso, tasmā duppaññoti vuccati. Kiñca nappajānāti? ‘Idaṁ <mark class="bg-yellow-100 dark:bg-yellow-900 rounded" style="padding-inline:0.05rem">dukkhan</mark>’ti napp

results.title: Udāna sutta - Inspired Saying
results.description: A teaching on the fearless resolve that severs the lower fetters, followed by the exact inquiry needed to immediately wear away mental defilements.
results.contentSnippet: <mark class="bg-yellow-100 dark:bg-yellow-900 rounded" style="padding-inline:0.05rem">Dukkhaṁ</mark> rūpaṁ ‘dukkhaṁ rūpan’ti yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti, dukkhaṁ vedanaṁ … dukkhaṁ saññaṁ … dukkhe saṅkhāre

results.title: Sabbāsava sutta - Overcoming Of All The Taints
results.description: The Buddha explains the cause for the restraint of all the taints and how there is abandoning of all the taints through the seven methods of seeing, restraint, proper use, enduring, avoiding, removing
results.contentSnippet: So ‘idaṁ <mark class="bg-yellow-100 dark:bg-yellow-900 rounded" style="padding-inline:0.05rem">dukkhan</mark>’ti yoniso manasi karoti, ‘ayaṁ dukkhasamudayo’ti yoniso manasi karoti, ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodho

results.title: Alagaddūpama sutta - Simile of the Water Snake
results.description: The Dhamma can be like a snake that bites if grasped wrongly. This discourse tackles the danger of misinterpretation, sparked by a bhikkhu who claimed sensual pleasures weren't obstructions. The Buddh
results.contentSnippet: “Kassa kho nāma tvaṁ, moghapurisa, mayā evaṁ dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānāsi? Nanu mayā, moghapurisa, anekapariyāyena antarāyikā dhammā antarāyikā vuttā? Alañca pana te paṭisevato antarāyāya. Appassādā kāmā v
